title: anthurium obtusifolium
intro: Anthurium obtusifolium, also known as the tailflower or flamingo flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Arum, native to Central and South America. The plant's medicinal properties come from its leaves, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of the plant include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain and improve overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the Anthurium obtusifolium are typically dried and made into teas or infusions, or used in tinctures and ointments to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
